headline: Why Government-Linked Companies Never Actually Reform
context: The EPU announced its fifth GLC transformation programme since 2005, targeting 37 entities controlling RM500B in assets, while board appointments remain political.

cards.big: Five reform programmes. Twenty years. Same boards.
cards.sub: GLC Transformation 1.0 through 5.0. Each declared the previous one incomplete.

cards.big: 68% of GLC board members have political affiliations.
cards.sub: Khazanah guidelines require independent directors. The definition of 'independent' is flexible.

cards.big: GLCs control 42% of KLCI market cap. Competition is structural fiction.
cards.sub: Telekom, Tenaga, Petronas subsidiaries — the market regulates around them, not through them.

cards.big: Every government since 2003 promised GLC reform. Every government appointed allies.
cards.sub: BN, PH, PN, Unity — the pattern transcends coalitions.

cards.big: Reform means fewer patronage positions. Who would vote for that?
cards.sub: The incentive structure defeats every reform programme before it launches.

cards.big: GLCs are not companies that need reform. They are the reform-proof architecture of Malaysian power.
